type1_summary: All three files appear to be Nature source-data PDFs, which in this paper context are most consistent with processed, figure-supporting, replot-ready material rather than raw STM instrument output.
type2_summary: No explicit raw instrument files (e.g., STM scan files, HDF5, proprietary microscope formats, or unprocessed measurement dumps) are present in the provided file list.
classification_notes: The paper itself clearly contains both raw STM measurements and processed source data, but the provided file list only includes Nature source-data PDFs. Because the internal contents were not inspected, the classification is conservative and based on repository/file context plus paper evidence. No Type 2 file is evident from the supplied files.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper states that source data are deposited and that figure-ready processed outputs include Fourier-filtered STM images, phase maps, amplitude maps, and bias-dependent curves. A Nature source-data PDF is consistent with figure-specific replot-ready material.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF source-data file, 6.1 MB, likely containing organized figure panels or extracted plots rather than raw instrument output. The size is moderate and consistent with compiled source data for multiple figures.
file_classifications.reasoning: Given the paper's explicit distinction between raw STM measurements and processed/source data, and the Nature source-data packaging, this file is most likely figure-oriented replot-ready data rather than raw instrument output. Conservatively classify as Type 1.
file_classifications.ambiguity: The internal contents were not directly inspected, so this is inferred from repository context and file type.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Likely figure-organized source data / embedded plots rather than tabular columns

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper reports processed STM-derived quantities and theoretical/simulation outputs as part of the deposited supporting data. Nature source-data PDFs are typically figure-specific deposits.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF source-data file, 3.7 MB, consistent with a compiled figure-supporting dataset or supplemental source-data package rather than raw scan files.
file_classifications.reasoning: The file is best interpreted as processed/source data intended to support figures and can likely be directly re-plotted or inspected with minimal processing. Type 1 is the conservative choice.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper emphasizes processed data products and theoretical calculations in the deposited supporting material. A smaller source-data PDF is consistent with a figure-specific supplement or calculation output.

file_classifications.reasoning: This file is most plausibly replot-ready source data or a compact supplemental figure/data sheet. No evidence suggests raw instrument output. Type 1 is appropriate.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies the charge density wave (CDW) in 2H-NbSe2 using bias-dependent scanning tunnelling microscopy (STM) topography combined with a simple 1D model and self-consistent multiband electronic-structure calculations. The key finding is evidence for a multiband CDW: in addition to the known gap near the Fermi level, the CDW also opens a gap on a second band, producing out-of-phase charge modulations that explain the observed bias-dependent CDW contrast in STM.
measurement_types: histogram-based phase/amplitude extraction from STM images

figures.description: Crystal structure of 2H-NbSe2 shown from top and side views, illustrating the layered trigonal prismatic structure and three-fold symmetry.
figures.likely_source: theoretical or drawn from known crystal structure

figures.description: Calculated Fermi surface from a two-band tight-binding fit to ARPES data, showing inner and outer pockets around Γ and K.
STM topography of NbSe2 at +50 mV showing atomic lattice and CDW modulation.
STM topography of the same region at -50 mV showing bias-dependent CDW contrast.
STM topography of the same region at -150 mV; note the image was corrected for diagonal running sharp lines for visualization.
figures.likely_source: experimental data with minor visualization correction

figures.description: Magnified STM image of the region marked in Fig. 2a.
Fourier-filtered CDW component corresponding to Fig. 2d.
Magnified STM image of the region marked in Fig. 2b.
Fourier-filtered CDW component corresponding to Fig. 2f.
Magnified STM image of the region marked in Fig. 2c.
Fourier-filtered CDW component corresponding to Fig. 2h.
Spatial map of the CDW dephasing parameter Θ(r) extracted from the STM image at +50 mV.
Spatial map of the CDW dephasing parameter Θ(r) extracted from the STM image at -50 mV.
Spatial map of the CDW dephasing parameter Θ(r) extracted from the STM image at -150 mV.
Bias dependence of the apparent CDW phase extracted from the dephasing parameter, showing a non-monotonic variation with bias.
Bias dependence of the CDW amplitude extracted from STM images.
Best-matching simulated phase dependence from the two-gap 1D model.
Best-matching simulated amplitude dependence from the two-gap 1D model.
1D model with a single charge modulation and a gap centered at the Fermi level; spatial and energy-dependent local DOS map.
Simulated topographic traces for the single-gap model at selected biases.
Bias dependence of phase and DOS for the single-gap model.
1D model with two charge modulations and two gaps at different energies, with no phase difference between modulations.
Simulated topographic traces for the two-gap in-phase model.
Bias dependence of phase and DOS for the two-gap in-phase model.
1D model with two charge modulations and a 120° phase shift between them.
Simulated topographic traces for the two-gap model with 120° phase shift.
Bias dependence of phase and DOS for the two-gap model with 120° phase shift, showing richer phase behavior.
Calculated density of states for inner and outer bands, comparing ungapped and gapped cases.
Difference between gapped and ungapped DOS, highlighting DOS suppressions at energies away from EF on both bands.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements are STM constant-current topographic scans of cleaved NbSe2 surfaces acquired at multiple sample biases between -0.5 V and 0.5 V at 1.2 K in UHV. The paper also references ARPES data from prior work as an input constraint for the band-structure model, but the present study’s directly collected raw data are the STM images.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The data supporting the findings have been deposited in the Yareta repository at https://doi.org/10.26037/yareta:en553fvpkrdorgqtssxegl4tyi. The paper also states that computer codes are available upon reasonable request and preferably within a collaboration.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described as STM images recorded in constant current mode with at least 64 pixel/nm resolution, using PtIr tips in UHV on in-situ cleaved NbSe2. The paper does not specify file formats in the text provided.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include Fourier-filtered CDW images, spatial maps of the dephasing parameter Θ(r), extracted phase values Θ0(Vb), extracted amplitudes A0(Vb), histograms of local phase/amplitude values, and normalized bias-dependent curves used for comparison with model calculations.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly implied for the main STM figures: the same STM region is shown at multiple biases, then Fourier-filtered, then converted into phase maps and bias-dependent extracted quantities. The paper does not explicitly say that source data files are attached per figure in the text, but the repository likely contains supporting data for the figures.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No explicit analysis notebooks or scripts are named in the text. The paper states that computer codes are available upon reasonable request, which indicates code exists but is not openly deposited in the text provided.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset mixes experimental STM scans with derived image-analysis products and theoretical/simulated curves. The main semantic distinction is between raw STM topographs, processed CDW phase/amplitude maps extracted by Fourier filtering and fitting, and simulated/model data from 1D and self-consistent multiband calculations. The paper also references supplementary figures and notes for details of the fitting and modeling procedures, suggesting additional derived data exist beyond the main figures.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section states that STM experiments were done in UHV and that STM images were recorded in constant current mode; the Results section discusses multiple topographic STM images at different biases.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper explicitly describes Fourier filtering, fitting of CDW contrast, extraction of dephasing parameter maps, histograms, and bias-dependent phase/amplitude curves, all of which are figure-ready processed data.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: "The data that support the ﬁndings of this study has been deposited in the Yareta repository" and "Computer codes are available upon reasonable request"; also "Details of the CDW amplitude and phase ﬁtting procedure can be found in ref 16."
